title: Obstruction Bridge FAIL: class 6 (forbidden, v_3=1) claimed reachable — FORBIDDEN_TARGET_REACHABILITY_CLAIM
_fixture_purpose: Canonical obstruction-ignored failure. The presenting party claims target_arithmetic_class=6 is reachable (claimed_reachable=true). But v_3(6)=1, so by [111] this class is forbidden — no norm-form element in Z[phi]/9Z[phi] can have norm 6. The obstruction_verdict is correctly set to OBSTRUCTION_PRESENT, but the claimed_reachable=true flag is inconsistent. This is the FORBIDDEN_TARGET_REACHABILITY_CLAIM error: a control cert that asserts it can drive a system to a target with arithmetic fingerprint 6 would be lying — there is no QA state (b,e) with f(b,e)≡6 mod 9. Any [106]-family PASS cert making such a claim would be structurally invalid. The bridge cert catches this inconsistency before a full plan/control cert is constructed.

bridge_claim.statement: INVALID CLAIM: arithmetic class 6 is claimed reachable, but v_3(6)=1 places it in the forbidden set of [111]. No element of Z[phi]/9Z[phi] has norm 6. No valid plan/control cert from [106] or its domain instances may assert a target with this arithmetic fingerprint.
bridge_claim.notes: The obstruction_verdict is correctly set (OBSTRUCTION_PRESENT), but the claimed_reachable flag contradicts it. This is an arithmetic-obstruction-ignored error.

validation_checks.details: OBSTRUCTION_PRESENT but claimed_reachable=true: INCONSISTENT — obstruction ignored; arithmetic class 6 cannot be the target of any valid plan/control witness

fail_ledger.message: target_arithmetic_class=6 has v_3(6)=1, placing it in the forbidden set {3,6} of [111] for p=3,k=2. No element of Z[phi]/9Z[phi] has norm ≡ 6 mod 9. The claimed_reachable=true assertion is inconsistent with obstruction_verdict='OBSTRUCTION_PRESENT'. Any plan/control cert from QA_PLAN_CONTROL_COMPILER_CERT.v1 or its domain instances that asserts reachability of a QA target state with arithmetic fingerprint 6 is structurally invalid — it would require a witness that cannot exist.
